name: GT-tildePhi-props
source: Kitaev arXiv:2405.02434, refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:2171-2182 (def tilde_Phi and its properties), VERBATIM: "\wt{\Phi}=\theta(2\Phi-1) =\frac{1}{2}\Bigl(1+\sgn(2\Phi-1)\Bigr) =\frac{1}{2}\Bigl(1+(2\Phi-1)\bigl(1-4(\Phi-\Phi^2)\bigr)^{-1/2}\Bigr)." and "The map $\wt{\Phi}$ has the properties" "\wt{\Phi}^2=\wt{\Phi},\qquad \|\wt{\Phi}-\Phi\|_\cb\le O(\eta),\qquad \wt{\Phi}(1)=1,\qquad \wt{\Phi}(X^\dag)=\wt{\Phi}(X)^\dag\text{ for all }X\in\Bo(\calH)". Applied to F: Ftilde=theta(2F-1), with Ftilde^2=Ftilde, ||Ftilde-F||_cb<=O(eta), Ftilde(1)=1, Ftilde(X^dag)=Ftilde(X)^dag. The Taylor expansion converges since eta<1/4 (GT-hyp). Note ||.||<=||.||_cb on a single-operator argument, so ||(Ftilde-F)(Z)||<=||Ftilde-F||_cb ||Z||<=O(eta)||Z||.
